title: Big Tech earnings to highlight critical 2027 capex levels: Gene Munster
excerpt: Capex is the main event for tech investors heading into major earnings reports. Gene Munster says the 2027 outlook is critical for the AI narrative.
content_html:
A veteran investor says this is the most important AI capex number that markets should watch

By Samuel O'Brient

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Big Tech earnings are here, and the AI capex discussion is set to be the main event for investors.

Rather than focusing on what the current quarter—or even the rest of 2026—may hold, tech veteran Gene Munster says investors should be looking further out.

With the AI trade looking "tired," the investor says that Q2 earnings are the only thing that can generate fresh momentum. Munster, a longtime tech analyst and now managing partner at Deepwater Asset Management, says he'll be focused most on what hyperscalers are planning for 2027.

"Within the hyperscaler capex conversation, the key metric is next year's growth," Munster wrote. "As it stands, the Street is looking for 23% growth in CY27. That said, whisper expectations have moved higher following Google's $85B and Amazon's $25B capital raises, which will be spent predominantly late this year and into next year."

Munster charted the four major hyperscalers' projected capex estimates for the coming year, and predicts that spending likely won't cool as much as Wall Street anticipates, leaving a large part of the bull thesis intact.

Munster noted that much of his analysis centers on the themes of AI capex spending and cloud computing in Q2 tech earnings, which he described as equally important for investors. But while he made it clear that he sees Microsoft as the weakest link in the hyperscaler chain, even its 20% year-to-date decline isn't enough to derail the AI infrastructure boom.

"The biggest narrative risk in the capex conversation is Microsoft, where I believe management will guide investors to an unchanged 26% growth rate for next year," Munster noted. "If three of the four hyperscalers raise their outlook, I believe the AI infrastructure narrative will remain intact."

Munster says that these stocks look cheap relative to recent earnings. He cited Nvidia as an example of a company that hasn't grown as quickly as Wall Street anticipated, resulting in its valuation multiples being pushed lower, from trading at 30x earnings projections last year to 16x 2027 estimates today.

From Munster's perspective, valuation compression is a natural step for these companies, as colossal growth of past quarters becomes harder to sustain.

But that doesn't mean investors should be worried, and earnings could be the update the bulls need to resume the rally.

"I expect earnings to continue growing by more than 20% annually for the next several years," he said. "If that proves true, it would lay the groundwork for AI infrastructure stocks to continue working, as earnings growth more than offsets the decline in valuation multiples."

Samuel O'Brient

Samuel O'Brient is an experienced financial markets and business journalist who has written extensively on a wide range of topics involving economics, technology and public policy. At Business Insider, he covers important macro and micro economic stories, including takes from leading economists and hedge fund managers, breaking IPOs,  corporate bankruptcies, meme stocks and short-selling. He also writes on other markets such as crypto, oil and real estate.He has interviewed many of the market’s most influential voices, ranging from top economists such as Mark Zandi and Richard Thalerto prominent investors including Danny Moses, Andrew Left, Anthony Scaramucci, Louis Navellier and Grant Cardone.Programs such as LiveNOW from Fox , Taking Stock and Ticker News have had Samuel on to discuss stock market and economic developments. His reporting has been cited by The New York Times DealBook, Bloomberg Radio, Forbes, Entrepreneur, Gizmodo and TheFutureParty.Samuel began at InvestorPlace, covering investing, retail trading and macro economic trends. Prior to joining Business Insider,  he served as a technology markets reporter at TheStreet. He is a graduate of Sarah Lawrence College and Trinity College Dublin.Samuel's work has appeared in publications such as TipRanks, EV and Observer. When he isn't chasing down stories, he can often be found browsing book and record shops. To reach Samuel, email him at sobrient@insider.com or connect with him on LinkedIn. He is also on Signal as Samuel Clemens. Popular Articles: A Nobel economist has a warning for meme stock tradersThe business school dropout who kicked off the Beyond Meat rally wants you to know he's not Roaring Kitty 2.0A top economist who thinks we're on the brink of a recession says he's eyeing these 3 warning signsTrump's 401(k) executive order marks big changes for retirement savings — and possibly puts your money at riskWhy hedge fund icon Ray Dalio says you shouldn't invest in real estate in this economyAI bullishness is soaring, but pros see a major opportunity brewing in an overlooked corner of the market

blocks.html_en: Big Tech earnings are here, and the AI capex discussion is set to be the main event for investors.

sentences.en: Big Tech earnings are here, and the AI capex discussion is set to be the main event for investors.
sentences.zh: 大型科技公司的财报已经发布，人工智能资本支出的讨论将成为投资者的主要活动。

blocks.html_en: Rather than focusing on what the current quarter—or even the rest of 2026—may hold, tech veteran Gene Munster says investors should be looking further out.

sentences.en: Rather than focusing on what the current quarter—or even the rest of 2026—may hold, tech veteran Gene Munster says investors should be looking further out.
sentences.zh: 科技资深人士吉恩·蒙斯特 (Gene Munster) 表示，投资者不应关注当前季度甚至 2026 年剩余时间的情况，而应放眼更远的地方。

blocks.html_en: With the AI trade looking "tired," the investor says that Q2 earnings are the only thing that can generate fresh momentum. Munster, a longtime tech analyst and now managing partner at Deepwater Asset Management, says he'll be focused most on what hyperscalers are planning for 2027.

sentences.en: With the AI trade looking "tired," the investor says that Q2 earnings are the only thing that can generate fresh momentum.
sentences.zh: 这位投资者表示，由于人工智能行业看起来“疲倦”，第二季度的收益是唯一能够产生新动力的因素。

sentences.en: Munster, a longtime tech analyst and now managing partner at Deepwater Asset Management, says he'll be focused most on what hyperscalers are planning for 2027.
sentences.zh: 长期担任技术分析师、现任 Deepwater Asset Management 管理合伙人的 Munster 表示，他将最关注超大规模企业 2027 年的计划。

blocks.html_en: "Within the hyperscaler capex conversation, the key metric is next year's growth," Munster wrote. "As it stands, the Street is looking for 23% growth in CY27. That said, whisper expectations have moved higher following Google's $85B and Amazon's $25B capital raises, which will be spent predominantly late this year and into next year."

sentences.en: "Within the hyperscaler capex conversation, the key metric is next year's growth," Munster wrote.
sentences.zh: “在超大规模资本支出对话中，关键指标是明年的增长，”蒙斯特写道。

sentences.en: "As it stands, the Street is looking for 23% growth in CY27.
sentences.zh: “就目前情况而言，华尔街预计 2027 年将实现 23% 的增长。

sentences.en: That said, whisper expectations have moved higher following Google's $85B and Amazon's $25B capital raises, which will be spent predominantly late this year and into next year."
sentences.zh: 也就是说，在 Google 的 85B 美元和亚马逊的 25B 美元融资之后，人们的预期已经走高，这些资金将主要用于今年年底和明年。”

blocks.html_en: Munster charted the four major hyperscalers' projected capex estimates for the coming year, and predicts that spending likely won't cool as much as Wall Street anticipates, leaving a large part of the bull thesis intact.

sentences.en: Munster charted the four major hyperscalers' projected capex estimates for the coming year, and predicts that spending likely won't cool as much as Wall Street anticipates, leaving a large part of the bull thesis intact.
sentences.zh: 蒙斯特绘制了四大超大规模企业对来年资本支出的预测，并预测支出可能不会像华尔街预期的那样降温，从而使牛市理论的大部分内容保持不变。

sentences.en: An earnings chart provided by Munster in his analysis on his web page.
sentences.zh: 蒙斯特在其网页上的分析中提供了收益图表。

sentences.en: GeneMunster.com

blocks.html_en: Munster noted that much of his analysis centers on the themes of AI capex spending and cloud computing in Q2 tech earnings, which he described as equally important for investors. But while he made it clear that he sees Microsoft as the weakest link in the hyperscaler chain, even its 20% year-to-date decline isn't enough to derail the AI infrastructure boom.

sentences.en: Munster noted that much of his analysis centers on the themes of AI capex spending and cloud computing in Q2 tech earnings, which he described as equally important for investors.
sentences.zh: 蒙斯特指出，他的大部分分析都集中在第二季度科技收益中的人工智能资本支出和云计算主题，他认为这对投资者同样重要。

sentences.en: But while he made it clear that he sees Microsoft as the weakest link in the hyperscaler chain, even its 20% year-to-date decline isn't enough to derail the AI infrastructure boom.
sentences.zh: 不过，尽管他明确表示，他认为微软是超大规模企业链中最薄弱的一环，但即使其今年迄今下降 20%，也不足以破坏人工智能基础设施的繁荣。

blocks.html_en: "The biggest narrative risk in the capex conversation is Microsoft, where I believe management will guide investors to an unchanged 26% growth rate for next year," Munster noted. "If three of the four hyperscalers raise their outlook, I believe the AI infrastructure narrative will remain intact."

sentences.en: "The biggest narrative risk in the capex conversation is Microsoft, where I believe management will guide investors to an unchanged 26% growth rate for next year," Munster noted.
sentences.zh: 蒙斯特指出：“资本支出对话中最大的叙述风险是微软，我相信微软管理层将引导投资者保持明年 26% 的增长率不变。”

sentences.en: "If three of the four hyperscalers raise their outlook, I believe the AI infrastructure narrative will remain intact."
sentences.zh: “如果四家超大规模企业中的三家提高了他们的前景，我相信人工智能基础设施的叙述将保持不变。”

blocks.html_en: Munster says that these stocks look cheap relative to recent earnings. He cited Nvidia as an example of a company that hasn't grown as quickly as Wall Street anticipated, resulting in its valuation multiples being pushed lower, from trading at 30x earnings projections last year to 16x 2027 estimates today.

sentences.en: Munster says that these stocks look cheap relative to recent earnings.
sentences.zh: 蒙斯特表示，与最近的收益相比，这些股票看起来很便宜。

sentences.en: He cited Nvidia as an example of a company that hasn't grown as quickly as Wall Street anticipated, resulting in its valuation multiples being pushed lower, from trading at 30x earnings projections last year to 16x 2027 estimates today.
sentences.zh: 他以英伟达为例，该公司的增长速度没有华尔街预期的那么快，导致其估值倍数被压低，从去年盈利预测的 30 倍降至如今 2027 年盈利预测的 16 倍。

blocks.html_en: From Munster's perspective, valuation compression is a natural step for these companies, as colossal growth of past quarters becomes harder to sustain.

sentences.en: From Munster's perspective, valuation compression is a natural step for these companies, as colossal growth of past quarters becomes harder to sustain.
sentences.zh: 从蒙斯特的角度来看，估值压缩对这些公司来说是一个自然的步骤，因为过去几个季度的巨大增长变得更难以维持。

blocks.html_en: But that doesn't mean investors should be worried, and earnings could be the update the bulls need to resume the rally.

sentences.en: But that doesn't mean investors should be worried, and earnings could be the update the bulls need to resume the rally.
sentences.zh: 但这并不意味着投资者应该担心，盈利可能是多头恢复涨势所需的最新信息。

blocks.html_en: "I expect earnings to continue growing by more than 20% annually for the next several years," he said. "If that proves true, it would lay the groundwork for AI infrastructure stocks to continue working, as earnings growth more than offsets the decline in valuation multiples."

sentences.en: "I expect earnings to continue growing by more than 20% annually for the next several years," he said.
sentences.zh: “我预计未来几年收入将继续以每年 20% 以上的速度增长，”他说。

sentences.en: "If that proves true, it would lay the groundwork for AI infrastructure stocks to continue working, as earnings growth more than offsets the decline in valuation multiples."
sentences.zh: “如果这是真的，这将为人工智能基础设施股票继续发挥作用奠定基础，因为盈利增长足以抵消估值倍数的下降。”

content_html_zh:
一位资深投资者表示，这是市场应该关注的最重要的人工智能资本支出数字

作者：Samuel O'Brient 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

大型科技公司的财报已经发布，人工智能资本支出的讨论将成为投资者的主要活动。

科技资深人士吉恩·蒙斯特 (Gene Munster) 表示，投资者不应关注当前季度甚至 2026 年剩余时间的情况，而应放眼更远的地方。

这位投资者表示，由于人工智能行业看起来“疲倦”，第二季度的收益是唯一能够产生新动力的因素。 长期担任技术分析师、现任 Deepwater Asset Management 管理合伙人的 Munster 表示，他将最关注超大规模企业 2027 年的计划。

“在超大规模资本支出对话中，关键指标是明年的增长，”蒙斯特写道。 “就目前情况而言，华尔街预计 2027 年将实现 23% 的增长。 也就是说，在 Google 的 85B 美元和亚马逊的 25B 美元融资之后，人们的预期已经走高，这些资金将主要用于今年年底和明年。”

蒙斯特绘制了四大超大规模企业对来年资本支出的预测，并预测支出可能不会像华尔街预期的那样降温，从而使牛市理论的大部分内容保持不变。

蒙斯特指出，他的大部分分析都集中在第二季度科技收益中的人工智能资本支出和云计算主题，他认为这对投资者同样重要。 不过，尽管他明确表示，他认为微软是超大规模企业链中最薄弱的一环，但即使其今年迄今下降 20%，也不足以破坏人工智能基础设施的繁荣。

蒙斯特指出：“资本支出对话中最大的叙述风险是微软，我相信微软管理层将引导投资者保持明年 26% 的增长率不变。” “如果四家超大规模企业中的三家提高了他们的前景，我相信人工智能基础设施的叙述将保持不变。”

蒙斯特表示，与最近的收益相比，这些股票看起来很便宜。 他以英伟达为例，该公司的增长速度没有华尔街预期的那么快，导致其估值倍数被压低，从去年盈利预测的 30 倍降至如今 2027 年盈利预测的 16 倍。

从蒙斯特的角度来看，估值压缩对这些公司来说是一个自然的步骤，因为过去几个季度的巨大增长变得更难以维持。

但这并不意味着投资者应该担心，盈利可能是多头恢复涨势所需的最新信息。

“我预计未来几年收入将继续以每年 20% 以上的速度增长，”他说。 “如果这是真的，这将为人工智能基础设施股票继续发挥作用奠定基础，因为盈利增长足以抵消估值倍数的下降。”

塞缪尔·奥布莱恩 (Samuel O'Brient) 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

塞缪尔·奥布莱恩 (Samuel O'Brient) 是一位经验丰富的金融市场和商业记者，撰写的文章涉及经济、技术和公共政策等广泛主题。 在《商业内幕》中，他报道了重要的宏观和微观经济故事，包括顶尖经济学家和对冲基金经理的观点、首次公开募股失败、企业破产、迷因股票和卖空。 他还撰写有关加密货币、石油和房地产等其他市场的文章。他采访了许多市场上最有影响力的声音，其中包括马克·赞迪（Mark Zandi）和理查德·泰勒托（Richard Thalerto）等顶级经济学家，以及丹尼·摩西（Danny Moses）、安德鲁·莱夫特（Andrew Left）、安东尼·斯卡拉穆奇（Anthony Scaramucci）、路易斯·纳维利尔（Louis Navellier）和格兰特·卡登（Grant Cardone）等著名投资者。福克斯的 LiveNOW、Taking Stock 和 Ticker News 等节目都邀请塞缪尔讨论股市和经济发展。 他的报道曾被纽约时报 DealBook、彭博电台、福布斯、Entrepreneur、Gizmodo 和 TheFutureParty 引用。Samuel 最初在 InvestorPlace 工作，报道投资、零售交易和宏观经济趋势。 在加入 Business Insider 之前，他曾担任 TheStreet 的技术市场记者。 他毕业于莎拉·劳伦斯学院和都柏林三一学院。Samuel 的作品曾发表在 TipRanks、EV 和 Observer 等出版物上。 当他不追寻故事时，人们经常会发现他在逛书店和唱片店。 要联系 Samuel，请发送电子邮件至 sobrient@insider.com 或通过 LinkedIn 与他联系。 他还在《信号》中饰演塞缪尔·克莱门斯。 热门文章： 一位诺贝尔经济学家向 meme 股票交易员发出警告 发起 Beyond Meat 集会的商学院辍学者希望你知道他不是 Roaring Kitty 2.0 一位认为我们正处于衰退边缘的顶级经济学家表示，他正在关注这 3 个警告信号 特朗普的 401(k) 行政命令标志着退休储蓄的重大变化 - 可能会让你的钱面临风险 为什么对冲基金偶像雷·达利奥 (Ray Dalio) 说你不应该投资房地产在当前经济形势下，人工智能的乐观情绪正在飙升，但专业人士看到了市场中被忽视的角落正在酝酿的重大机遇
